Feed post

malika sharma

• 3rd+

Founder @Lore | A Social Commerce Platform reimagining how we discover and shop.

5d •

The industry isn't fair to D2C brands.
Here's what I mean.
80% of people in India discover what they want to buy on Instagram. They're scrolling, getting inspired every single day.
But 70% only actually buy on marketplaces like Myntra and Amazon, not on D2C websites, because they prefer the trust, convenience, and pricing that marketplaces offer.
So think about what that does to a small brand.
You build a beautiful product. You post it on Instagram. Someone discovers it, loves it, clicks your link.
And then they leave, because they'd rather buy on a marketplace they already trust.
So you're stuck with two bad options.
Sell on your own website and face low conversions, high RTO because people default to COD, and rising CAC from showing Meta ads to cold traffic that never converts.
Or sell on a marketplace and hand over 20 to 40 percent commission while becoming just another faceless listing.
Either way, the brand loses.
This is the problem I think about every day. It's why I'm building Lore.
More on what we're building soon.


#D2C #ecommerce #buildinpublic #startupindia

Feed post

Mimi Turner

• 3rd+

Head of Marketplace Innovation, LinkedIn

Promoted by LinkedIn

✅ The rules of competition have changed. Winning strategy used to be simple: be the biggest or be the cheapest. But in a world of Answer Engine Optimization, another option is opening up - being the most relevant, most recommended and most validated. These are the signals that get you surfaced and considered in a way that compresses traditional discovery. This potentially creates new opportunity: small brands that contour their content around specific buyer questions can appear in LLM answers and shift consideration sets, while legacy brands who are used to being considered automatically MIGHT not appear if their content doesn't surface them in the same way. Being Buyable and being Discoverable operate on the same signal set, consensus signals of authority, trust and relatability - that's what makes brands safe to buy. Read our blog (link in comments below) to learn more about how to win in the age of AI driven discovery.

      Feed post

      Hardik Malik likes this

      Bhumi Sharma

      • 2nd

      B.Tech CSE Graduate ’26 | Intern @ Nokia | Building AI Agents & LLM Apps | Sharing What I Learn

      1d •

      Why would any company even hire me when AI can already code better than me??🤔

      This question used to bother me so much when I was in college and had no clue how corporate life actually worked

      Every week there is a new AI tool and it is doing things I spent months trying to learn --

      - Writing code faster than me and even cleaner
      - Cracking LeetCode like it is absolutely nothing
      - Explaining system design better than some of my professors ever did

      So the fear felt very real. Why would anyone even hire me?😭

      Then I got my internship and slowly started understanding the answer

      because nobody came to me and said "here, just write some code"

      They asked me to:

      - First understand the problem deeply
      - Think through different approaches
      - Weigh the trade-offs
      - Make a call on which solution actually makes sense for this specific situation
      - Communicate that decision to the whole team

      that whole messy thinking process in the middle?

      AI can assist but it cannot replace the human judgment behind it.

      AI can give you 5 solutions in seconds. But figuring out which one is right for your specific problem, your specific team, your specific constraints - that still needs a human brain behind it

      AI generates code.

      But it does not understand why you need it.
      It does not question whether you should build it at all.
      It does not know your users, your business, your constraints, or the decisions that led to this moment.
      It just executes whatever you tell it to.

      The thinking, the judgment, the back and forth with your team, the uncomfortable questions nobody wants to raise - that is still entirely on you.

      The better AI gets at coding, the more important human judgment becomes

      Someone still has to think
      Someone still has to decide
      Someone still has to question
      Someone still has to take responsibility

      AI is the assistant. You are the engineer.

      And that is when it finally clicked for me-

      Engineering was never really about writing code. It was always about solving the right problems. Code is just one of the tools we use to get there.

      And if you are someone who was scared AI would replace you - hi, same. This is for you 🙂

      I still have a lot to learn, but this perspective makes me excited about the future instead of worried about it.

      drop a comment if this fear has ever hit you too
      would love to know how you think about it 🙃


      #ArtificialIntelligence #SoftwareEngineering #AI #CareerGrowth #TechCareers #WomenInTech #Engineering #InternLife #FutureOfWork #LearningInPublic

        Feed post

        Shubham Misal likes this

        Software Developer

        Rasikh Ahmed K. • 3rd+

        2w •

        ⚙️ 𝐀𝐈 𝐜𝐚𝐧 𝐰𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐞 𝟏𝟎,𝟎𝟎𝟎 𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐜𝐨𝐝𝐞 𝐟𝐚𝐬𝐭.

        But speed is not the same as quality.
        More code can also mean:
        ✅ more bugs
        ✅ more bloat
        ✅ more security risks
        ✅ more maintenance
        ✅ more technical debt

        AI is a powerful accelerator.
        But it still needs engineering discipline:
        Review it.
        Test it.
        Refactor it.
        Secure it.
        Understand it.

        Because "it generated quickly" does not mean "it belongs in production."
        Good developers use AI to move faster.
        Great developers verify before they ship. 📌

        🔁 Repost if this resonates.
        📌 Save for later.
        👥 Follow Rasikh Ahmed K. for more AI and software development insights.

        #AICoding #CodeQuality #SoftwareEngineering #DeveloperHumor

        Feed post

        Suggested

        Yash Rawat

        • 3rd+

        AI Engineer & Systems Architect | 4+ yrs in LLM/RAG, Multi-Agent, RLHF & MCP Apps | Building MemoryStore.in

        2d •

        You thought AI mirror tests were sci-fi.
        This robot just passed it with flying colors.

        The journey from identifying a common utensil to recognizing one's own reflection is a fundamental cognitive milestone. For this AI, it happened in moments.

        Initially, it effortlessly identified a fork. But when presented with a mirror, its response evolved from describing a 'sleek metallic surface' to a sudden, emphatic 'OH! It's ME!' followed by a self-compliment.

        This isn't just a party trick. It's a fascinating glimpse into how quickly advanced AI models are developing new capabilities, even those hinting at self-awareness. The ability to distinguish self from environment, even through reflection, is a significant leap. It challenges our timelines for artificial general intelligence and raises profound questions about the nature of consciousness itself.

        Does self-recognition truly mark the dawn of AI consciousness?

        #AI
        #ArtificialIntelligence
        #SelfAwareness
        #AGI
        #FutureofTech

        Thanks to ai.rise.co for sharing this video 🙏
